evidence: JSpecify @Nullable/@NonNull used consistently at all public API boundaries: ExplorationService interface, QueryExecutor interface, ExploreResponseAssembler utility methods all carry full annotations

CollocateResult.rawXml typed as non-nullable String (no @Nullable) yet is null-checked defensively in three locations in ExploreResponseAssembler: lines 195, 203, 242 — type promises a String, runtime code treats it as Optional
CollocateResult.collocateLemma correctly annotated @Nullable and guarded in the same assembler — consistent treatment showing the rawXml gap is unintentional

Map<String, Object> used for all HTTP response bodies (60 occurrences codebase-wide): key names are string literals with no compile-time checking, value types are Object — all of ExploreResponseAssembler, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers build responses this way
CorpusQueryHandlers uses a private BcqlQueryResponse record for one endpoint — typed serialisation is possible but applied inconsistently
Internal data transfer records are well-typed: CollocateSearch, AggregatedCollocateStats, SeedCollocateData, BcqlRequest, ConcordanceExamplesRequest — request-side is consistently typed
RelationConfig.relationType() returns Optional<RelationType> — correct use of Optional for an absent-by-design field rather than @Nullable

BlackLabSnippetParser annotates multiple return types @Nullable (lines 30, 61, 136, 151, 176, 194, 207) and callers check for null, showing the pattern is understood and applied correctly throughout parsing code
CollocateQueryHelper.index field is @Nullable with a requireIndex() guard throwing IllegalStateException — the field-level annotation matches the runtime invariant

strengths: JSpecify @Nullable/@NonNull annotations applied consistently across all public-API interfaces (ExplorationService, QueryExecutor) and utility classes, giving callers accurate nullability contracts
Internal data-pipeline records (CollocateSearch, AggregatedCollocateStats, SeedCollocateData) are strongly typed, keeping the algorithmic core type-safe without Map<String, Object> leakage
Optional<RelationType> used correctly for an absent-by-design field rather than a nullable primitive, demonstrating awareness of when Optional is appropriate
@Nullable on BlackLabSnippetParser return values is precise and respected by callers — the parsing subsystem is internally consistent
type_safety.issue_character: The issues are split between a single concrete annotation gap (rawXml unannotated despite null runtime behaviour) and a systemic pattern of untyped Map<String, Object> for all HTTP response assembly — the former is a localised discrepancy, the latter is an architectural choice that forfeits compile-time key-name and value-type safety across the entire API layer.
type_safety.score_rationale: The codebase sits above 60 because null-safety discipline at the API boundary is genuinely strong: interfaces are fully annotated, model records carry @Nullable where needed, and internal records replace loose maps in the processing pipeline. It falls below 80 because the response-assembly layer, which is large (60 Map<String,Object> occurrences across ExploreResponseAssembler, ExplorationHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers), uses untyped string-keyed maps throughout, losing compile-time safety for every JSON key name and value type in every response. The rawXml gap — a String field null-checked in three places with no annotation — is a smaller but concrete annotation-reality mismatch. Together these represent significant drag from a type-discipline perspective, but the strong annotation coverage elsewhere prevents a lower score.

issues.summary: CollocateResult.rawXml typed as non-null String but null-checked in 3 places in ExploreResponseAssembler
evidence: QueryResults.java line 48: `public record CollocateResult(String sentence, String rawXml, ...` — rawXml has no @Nullable annotation
ExploreResponseAssembler.java line 195: `m.put("raw", r.rawXml() != null ? r.rawXml() : "")` — defensive null check contradicts the non-null type
Same pattern at lines 203 and 242 in the same file
By contrast, collocateLemma on the same record is correctly annotated @Nullable and also guarded
issues.suggestion: Add @Nullable to the rawXml field in the CollocateResult record declaration, matching the treatment of collocateLemma. This makes the null-checks in ExploreResponseAssembler honest rather than defensive dead-code guards that happen to be necessary.

issues.summary: All HTTP response bodies assembled as Map<String, Object> with string literal keys, forfeiting compile-time type safety across the entire API layer
evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler has 12+ methods each building HashMap<String, Object> with string literal keys ("word", "log_dice", "shared_by_count", etc.); a typo in any key is invisible until the client receives a wrong JSON field

ExplorationHandlers.buildSingleSeedResponseEnvelope and buildMultiSeedResponseEnvelope also build Map<String, Object> — the same response shape is assembled in multiple places with no shared type to enforce consistency
CorpusQueryHandlers.handleCorpusQuery uses a typed BcqlQueryResponse record instead — demonstrating the pattern is feasible but unevenly applied
60 occurrences of Map<String, Object> across the api package vs approximately 5 typed record usages
issues.suggestion: Introduce typed response records (e.g., ExploreResponse, SeedCollocateEntry, CoreCollocateEntry) for the repeated response shapes in ExploreResponseAssembler. Jackson will serialize them automatically. This aligns the response layer with the already-typed request parsing layer (BcqlRequest, ConcordanceExamplesRequest) and catches key-name discrepancies at compile time.

root_causes: Response assembly was built incrementally as the API surface grew, defaulting to the zero-friction Map<String, Object> pattern rather than committing to typed records for the output side — the request side was typed correctly as it was built later
rawXml nullability was left unannotated, likely because the field was added before the @Nullable convention was established for record components
likely_symptoms: response_map_untyped is a systemic pattern rather than isolated, but individual manifestations are many small put() calls rather than one structural defect
